steps.title: Fastest path: mark the angles first
steps.content: Track the given angle relationships first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Set up
steps.content: Triangle DEF ~ triangle PQR with D↔P, E↔Q, F↔R, so ∠R = ∠F. The interior angles of triangle DEF sum to 180°: $$∠D + ∠E + ∠F = 180^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Solve
steps.content: Substitute the given angle measures: $$47^{\circ} + 29^{\circ} + ∠F = 180^{\circ}$$ $$∠F = 180^{\circ} - 76^{\circ} = 104^{\circ}$$ Since ∠R = ∠F, the measure of angle R is 104°.
